Environmental Impact Statement, Los Angeles Harbor Deepening Project

The Los Angeles Harbor Deepening Project aims to deepen navigation channels and turning basins in Los Angeles Harbor, California. The dredged material will be used to create new land for port development. This project is expected to reduce the number of commercial vessels by accommodating larger ships, thus decreasing pollution. It will also increase docking facilities and remove polluted sediments. However, it may lead to the loss of marine habitats and a slight reduction in tidal flushing. The project includes a mitigation plan to protect endangered species and address environmental impacts.
Los Angeles Harbor, Los Angeles County, California
U.S. Army Corps of Engineers – Los Angeles District
California Department of Fish and Game

The Los Angeles Harbor Deepening Project was proposed to enhance the port's capacity for larger vessels, which would help streamline shipping operations and reduce pollution from smaller ships. While there was some public concern regarding the potential environmental impacts, particularly the loss of marine habitats, the project included mitigation measures to address these issues. Ultimately, the project aimed to balance economic growth with environmental protection.
